finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_08.md:223 (Spike turns glass instead of asking 'What kind' — non-clean reply breaks Q&A ladder); chapters/chapter_08.md:225 (Hector self-corrects: 'I mean real paperwork... Copies of — not a lot, but enough'); chapters/chapter_08.md:247 (Hector searches through multiple false starts recalling Lin codes: 'put a paper down with — the route codes... but those numbers were — anybody who worked the basin'); chapters/chapter_08.md:289 (Spike gives flat 'I can see that' instead of prompting next question); chapters/chapter_08.md:291 (Hector trails off: 'Not because she —'); chapters/chapter_08.md:297 (Hector trails off twice: 'luxury of —' and 'which is —' replacing the polished 'hobbies that doesn't age well' aphorism); chapters/chapter_08.md:349 (Hector's young-woman speech fragments with trail-off: 'circling the same —'); chapters/chapter_08.md:353 (Hector interrupts himself mid-speech: 'but she —' then resets to simpler close); chapters/chapter_08.md:365 (Spike drinks instead of asking 'Meaning' — Hector fills the silence); chapters/chapter_08.md:435 (Hector false-starts his final answer: 'I said —' then restarts: 'That they always —')
finding_results.notes: Broke the prompt-answer-explanation ladder at seven points across the Spike-Hector exchange. Non-clean replies from Spike at three points (lines 223, 289, 365) where he deflects physically or gives non-advancing responses instead of asking the next neat question. Hector self-corrects, trails off, or searches for wording in six turns (lines 225, 247, 291, 297, 349/353, 435). Removed the snap 'Useful' / 'You want useful, go ask a ledger' volley (former lines 305-307) that exemplified the clipped-question/clipped-answer/polished-corrective pattern. Removed two instances of narrator-admires-dialogue commentary ('That landed' at former line 189; 'The line landed closer to the truth than Spike liked' at former line 383). Preserved the conversation's information delivery, emotional arc, and all continuity payloads while roughening its surface into messier, more spoken texture.

summary: Revised the Spike-Hector exchange at The Radiant to break the recurring clean-ladder pattern of clipped question / clipped answer / polished add-on cited in ch08_dialogue_001. Introduced spoken roughness through Hector self-corrections, trail-offs, and false starts (6 instances across lines 225, 247, 291, 297, 349-353, 435) and through Spike deflections and non-advancing replies (3 instances at lines 223, 289, 365). Removed the 'Useful' / 'You want useful' snap exchange and two narrator-admires-dialogue instances ('landed'). The conversation now advances through messier, more realistic pressure cadence while preserving its emotional shape, information payload, and all continuity anchors.
